title: Tapeinosperma synneurum
intro: Tapeinosperma synneurum is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to Australia, particularly in the tropical and subtropical regions. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Tapeinosperma synneurum include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tapeinosperma synneurum is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a medicinal drink, and it can also be found in some commercial supplements.

This comprehensive classification reflects the plant's botanical identity.
